summary:
New onset proteinuria indicates true superimposition onto chronic hypertension; thrombocytopenia, increased blood pressures, and retinal changes are signs within an evolving condition but don't exclude underlying chronic status fully. Thus,

The exception indicating no clear sign of superimposed disease process among these options is \(\boxed{\text{retinal hypertensive changes}}\) due its more reflective nature toward ongoing chronic conditions versus acute complications suggesting full-blown superimposed pre-eclampsia. Agent2's final summary focuses clearly on identifying which feature doesn’t signal immediate superimposing risk despite other strong indications present for progressing pregnancy-related high-risk syndrome management concerns. 

---

Agent2's final summary focuses clearly on identifying which feature doesn’t signal immediate superimposing risk despite other strong indications present for progressing pregnancy-related high-risk syndrome management concerns.
